rationale: The cited paper is used as more than background: the citing study positions it as one of the key prior repeat-usage social influence studies (peer log-in behavior) when motivating its contribution. It is also used in the modeling section to justify temporal social-effect structure and to benchmark additional lag carryover via an exponential-smoothing specification similar to Trusov et al. These roles meaningfully inform both framing and empirical specification, not just a passing literature list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Trusov, Bodapati, and Bucklin (2010) as an empirical and methodological precedent for social influence in repeat online usage behavior. It builds on that foundation while extending to a wellness-program setting with monetary incentives and explicit controls for same-office contemporaneous correlation.
